use_cases: Responsively managing visible navigation items by hiding overflow into a 'more' indicator
Dynamically adjusting breadcrumb or tag displays based on container width
Creating responsive tab bars or toolbar menus that adapt to available space
Building avatar groups that show a '+N' overflow count when space is limited
Implementing collapsible item lists in sidebars or filter panels
ai_summary: A custom React hook that calculates and manages how many items can be visibly displayed within a container width, automatically hiding overflow items and providing a visible count. Uses ResizeObserver and getBoundingClientRect for accurate measurements. Ideal for responsive navigation bars, toolbars, tag clouds, and any list-based UI that needs adaptive item visibility across different screen sizes.
